articles.title: WTI climbs above $99.00 as Middle East tensions escalate, Trump threatens Iran over Strait of Hormuz
articles.expand: West Texas Intermediate (WTI), the US crude oil benchmark, is trading around $99.10 during the early European trading hours on Monday. The WTI price jumps amid an escalating conflict in the Middle East.

articles.title: USD/JPY Price Forecast: Approaches 160.00 as US Dollar gains amid Middle East war
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair trades 0.22% higher to near 159.60 during the European trading session on Monday. The pair gains as the US Dollar (USD) trades firmly amid firm demand for safe-haven assets, in the wake of the war in the Middle East.

articles.title: Euro: PMIs to show how Oil shock is affecting prices – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale’s macro team highlights that March flash PMIs will be the first key read on how the recent Oil shock is affecting Euro area activity and prices.

articles.title: Precious metals continue to bleed out as US-Iran conflict stirs up negative trifecta
articles.expand: Gold is down another 4% today as it closes in on $4,300 while silver is down 6% on the day as nears $64. It's starting to get rough out there as precious metals are hit by a trifecta of negative factors. The US-Iran conflict might have seemed like a good reason for precious metals to gain on geopolitical tensions. That was the case in January amid ...

articles.title: EUR/USD: Volatility signals questioned after energy shock – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Thu Lan Nguyen argues that despite what is described as the greatest threat to energy security in history, implied EUR/USD volatility remains unusually low.

articles.title: FX option expiries for 23 March 10am New York cut
articles.expand: There aren't any major expiries to take note of for the day, with the full list seen below. There is a large-ish one for EUR/USD at the 1.1600 mark but it likely won't factor much into play. That as the broader market sentiment right now is more focused on inflation fears as the Middle East conflict looks set to drag on further. US president Trump ...

articles.title: USD/CHF gathers strength to near 0.7990 on Fed hawkish tone
articles.expand: The USD/CHF pair gathers strength to near 0.7890 during the early European session on Monday. A hawkish hold from the US Federal Reserve (Fed) provides some support to the US Dollar (USD) against the Swiss Franc (CHF). Traders will closely monitor the situation in the Middle East.

articles.title: Yields continue to soar higher as inflation fears permeate across markets
articles.expand: It's a brand new week but the main focus in markets stay on the Middle East. The US-Iran conflict drags on further and the latest development is that US president Trump delivered a warning over the weekend for Iran to "reopen" the Strait of Hormuz. Trump gave a 48-hour ultimatum, which will run its course late in the day today. Iran has so far resp...

articles.title: NZD/USD Price Forecast: Bears dominate below 200-SMA barrier near 0.5865-0.5870
articles.expand: The NZD/USD pair attracts sellers for the second straight day and slides back closer to the 0.5800 mark during the Asian session on Monday. Moreover, the broader fundamental backdrop and technical setup suggest that the path of least resistance for spot prices remains to the downside.

articles.title: US Dollar Index edges higher above 99.50 on Middle East tensions, hawkish Fed
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY), an index of the value of the US Dollar (USD) measured against a basket of six world currencies, currently trades near 99.65 during the early European trading hours on Monday.

articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: Iran war to keep shaping the market’s sentiment
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair bounced back in the last few days, settling for the week around 1.1530. The Iran war and central banks’ monetary policy announcements took centre stage, yet none was enough to impress speculative interest.

articles.title: GBP/USD Weekly Forecast: Pound tests 200-day SMA as hawkish BoE fuels recovery
articles.expand: The Pound Sterling (GBP) staged a late rebound from near three-month lows against the US Dollar (USD), testing the critical 1.3440 supply zone.

articles.title: Gold Weekly Forecast: Energy prices, Fed commentary to continue to drive action
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) extended its slide into a third consecutive week and lost more than 6%, its worst weekly performance since March 2020, as major central banks adopted a hawkish tone because of the upside risks to inflation posed by rising energy prices.

articles.title: War in the Middle East accelerates away from the off-ramp
articles.expand: Threats and counter-threats of irrevocable action have markets on edge, and consumers saving their pennies to spend on higher fuel costs for the foreseeable.

articles.title: Home building industry facing COVID-like price spikes due to Middle East war
articles.expand: From sand to concrete, suppliers are bringing in "emergency fuel levies" as petrol prices soar due to the war in the Middle East. Major companies that supply pipes for plumbing are even warning of shortages.

articles.title: AFL Origin, Valentine’s Day a one-off spending sugar hit as Middle East conflict adds to cost-of-living crisis
articles.expand: West Aussies reached for their wallets at the AFL Origin and during Valentine’s Day in February, but one expert warns it’s a one-off spending sugar hit as the US-Iran war adds to the cost-of-living crisis.

articles.title: The 2 sectors Regal is betting on to ride out the tech wreck
articles.expand: Regal’s head of fundamental research Mark Nathan talks war, the oil shock and the beaten up stocks he has been buying that he says are safe from AI disruption.

articles.expand: Prime Minister Keir Starmer is expected to chair an emergency COBR meeting this week with top ministers on the cost of living crisis as Iran tensions threaten oil supplies and energy prices

articles.title: Keir Starmer in crisis cost-of-living summit as Ministers try to prevent panic-buying
articles.expand: The Prime Minister will convene Government colleagues and experts, following warnings that prices of petrol and food will rise due to the Iran war.

articles.expand: EUR/USD tests key level (1.1578) as the ECB's hawkish stance battles downside risks from Middle East conflict and spiking energy costs. The article analyzes the "April Hike" wildcard, NFP volatility, and critical technical levels. Outlook is split between technical recovery and fundamental risks.

articles.title: Chart alert: Watch 157.40 on USD/JPY, hawkish BoJ, ECB and BoE ignite yen strength
articles.expand: USD/JPY weakens as hawkish signals from the European Central Bank and Bank of England offset Fed expectations, while a firmer tone from the Bank of Japan supports yen strength. A break below 157.40 could trigger further downside toward key support levels.

articles.title: BOE Announced “Hawkish Hold” As Inflation Risks Rose on US-Iran War
articles.expand: The BOE voted unanimously to keep Bank Rate on hold at 3.75% at its March 2026 meeting thanks to a sharp spike in global energy prices triggered by conflict in the Middle East.

articles.expand: The People's Bank of China rate setting is a non-event. China's main policy rate is now the reverse repo rate, currently at 1.4% for the 7-day. The 7-day rate serves as a key policy benchmark, influencing other lending rates like the Loan Prime Rates (LPRs). articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Some consolidation likely before a move to 0.7200?
articles.expand: For the time being, the near-term prospect for the Australian Dollar (AUD) remains positive, supported by consistently high inflation in Australia and the RBA's hawkish stance.

articles.expand: El XAU/USD muestra corrección tras semanas de subidas previas. El fortalecimiento del dólar y la postura hawkish de la Fed generan presión vendedora. Movimientos macroeconómicos hoy podrían intensificar la volatilidad.

articles.expand: SNB holds rates but strengthens intervention stance, aiming to cap CHF gains as energy-driven inflation rises only temporarily.
